- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.

raw_response_text:
```xml
<condition>
    <example_analysis>
        <class_44>
            <visual_features>
                <feature>Deep purple petals with a velvety texture</feature>
                <feature>Prominent yellow-green stamens in the center</feature>
                <feature>Thick, rounded petal edges</feature>
            </visual_features>
        </class_44>
        <class_45>
            <visual_features>
                <feature>Bright orange petals with darker orange centers</feature>
                <feature>Small, tubular shape with slightly ruffled edges</feature>
                <feature>Greenish leaves visible in the background</feature>
            </visual_features>
        </class_45>
        <class_77>
            <visual_features>
                <feature>Large, delicate white or pink petals</feature>
                <feature>Flat, layered petals resembling a lotus or water lily</feature>
                <feature>Bright yellow stamens in the center</feature>
            </visual_features>
        </class_77>
    </example_analysis>
</condition>

<target_image_analysis>
    <comparison>
        <feature>Deep purple petals with a velvety texture</feature>
        <match>Yes</match>
    </comparison>
    <comparison>
        <feature>Thick, rounded petal edges</feature>
        <match>Yes</match>
    </comparison>
    <comparison>
        <feature>Prominent yellow-green stamens in the center</feature>
        <match>No (center appears to have dried or brownish elements)</match>
    </comparison>
</target_image_analysis>

<response>44</response>
```

```xml
<observations>
    <class_45>
        <features>
            <petal_color>orange with red spots</petal_color>
            <shape>smaller, tubular or bell-shaped</shape>
            <stamen>prominent, often with contrasting colors</stamen>
        </features>
    </class_45>
    <class_44>
        <features>
            <petal_color>deep purple or pinkish-purple</petal_color>
            <shape>broad, rounded petals with a slightly ruffled edge</shape>
            <stamen>yellowish, centrally located</stamen>
        </features>
    </class_44>
    <class_77>
        <features>
            <petal_color>pale pink to bright pink</petal_color>
            <shape>large, layered petals, symmetrical and flat</shape>
            <stamen>bright yellow center, prominent and round</stamen>
            <leaf>broad, green leaves visible</leaf>
        </features>
    </class_77>
</observations>

<target_analysis>
    <petal_color>pale pink to bright pink</petal_color>
    <shape>large, layered petals, symmetrical and flat</shape>
    <stamen>bright yellow center, prominent and round</stamen>
    <leaf>broad green leaves visible</leaf>
</target_analysis>

<response>77</response>
```

1. Analyze the provided labeled examples to identify the observable visual features that distinguish each class.

3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Target Image.

# INSTRUCTIONS
- Base your decision only on observable visual evidence in the examples and the Target Image.
